overview: The Wiki Filter extracts translatable text from wiki markup. Currently the only supported markup style is [DokuWiki](https://www.dokuwiki.org/dokuwiki). It extracts text from headers, paragraphs, list items, image captions, and table cells/headers, treating wiki formatting syntax as inline codes.

preserve_whitespace.help:
Prevents the filter from collapsing whitespace in the extracted content. Set to `true` to keep all original whitespace intact.

Configure in YAML as:
```yaml
preserve_whitespace: true
```

simplifierRules.help: Defines rules for simplifying how inline codes are represented in extracted segments. Use to reduce complexity of inline code placeholders for translators.

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.help: Inline codes that appear at the very beginning or end of a segment are moved out of the translatable content and into the skeleton. This simplifies segments for translators when boundary codes don't affect the translatable text.

mergeAdjacentCodes.help: When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no translatable text between them, they are combined into a single placeholder code. This reduces clutter in segments that have consecutive formatting or markup tags.

limitations: Attributes of inline codes (link and image URLs, etc.) are not exposed for translation or special processing.
Embedded HTML, PHP, etc., is not extracted.
processingNotes: If the file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark, the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used; otherwise the default encoding specified when opening the document is used.
All DokuWiki syntax as described at dokuwiki.org/syntax is supported for inline codes.

examples.title: DokuWiki markup extraction
examples.description: The filter extracts translatable text (highlighted below) from standard DokuWiki structures: headers, paragraphs, list items, image captions, and table cells.
examples.input:
```wiki
=== Header ===
Paragraph
 * List item
  * List item

{{image.jpg|Image caption}}

^ Table header 1 ^ Table header 2 |
| Table cell 1 | Table cell 2 |
```

examples.title: Custom inline codes configuration
examples.description: Define custom inline codes using regex patterns. Use `pattern` for standalone placeholders, or `start_pattern`/`end_pattern` for paired opening/closing tags.
